library_preparation_preflight.summary: No extra Maven dependency, Docker image, or environment/system property is needed. The artifact depends transitively on its Maven SCM, Plexus, SLF4J, Commons IO/Text/Lang, and javax.inject runtime dependencies, and existing meaningful coverage can exercise command-line construction and output consumers without additional setup. The javac failure is caused by Maven SCM 2.2.1 API changes, not missing setup.
risks: Tests that choose to execute real Git operations, rather than just command construction or consumers, will rely on a git executable being available on PATH and should use temporary local repositories/remotes to avoid network access.
If tests execute commits or signed tags, they may need local repository git configuration such as user.name/user.email or should avoid operations requiring global Git/GPG configuration.
The 2.2.1 compile failure must be fixed by adapting to the changed GitTagCommand API: SignOption replaces the old boolean tag signing argument, and createPushCommandLine is no longer static.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8354 [Automation] javac compile fails for org.apache.maven.scm:maven-scm-provider-gitexe:2.2.1; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015
